name: Kurukshetra
description: Kurukshetra, meaning 'the land of the Kurus,' is a historically and religiously significant region in the state of Haryana, India. It holds immense importance in Hindu mythology as the site of the epic battle of the Mahabharata, a war fought between the Pandavas and the Kauravas for the throne of Hastinapur. The Bhagavad Gita, a sacred Hindu scripture containing the philosophical discourse between Arjuna and Lord Krishna, was revealed on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. This makes the land a pivotal location for understanding Dharma (righteousness), Karma (action), and Moksha (liberation). The region is also associated with numerous ancient events and is considered a holy pilgrimage site, attracting devotees and scholars alike. The battle lasted for 18 days and resulted in a significant loss of life, marking a turning point in the history of the Kurus. The land is believed to be blessed and purified by the presence of Lord Krishna and the events that transpired there, making it a place of spiritual awakening and reflection. The Brahma Sarovar, Sannihit Sarovar, and Jyotisar are some of the important pilgrimage sites in Kurukshetra.
subtitle: Land of the Mahabharata and Bhagavad Gita

keyMyths.title: The Mahabharata Battle
keyMyths.description: The epic battle between the Pandavas and Kauravas took place on the plains of Kurukshetra, lasting for 18 days and resulting in a victory for Dharma. It was a war fought for righteousness and justice.

keyMyths.title: The Bhagavad Gita
keyMyths.description: Lord Krishna revealed the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, providing profound philosophical insights on duty, devotion, and liberation. This divine discourse is a central text in Hindu philosophy.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The epic poem detailing the Kurukshetra War and its aftermath.
sources.text: The epic poem detailing the Kurukshetra War and its aftermath.
